basic_details.job_title: UPSC Recruitment for Assistant Soil Chemist, Prosecutor, Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor & 10 Other Posts – Apply Online
basic_details.total_vacancies: 13 distinct posts (48 vacancies as per the individual vacancy counts listed in the notification)
basic_details.job_location: Varies by post - New Delhi and other locations as specified under each post's headquarters
basic_details.short_information: The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has released Advertisement No. 08/2026, inviting online applications for recruitment by selection to 13 posts spread across the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Ministry of Corporate Affairs, Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Ministry of Home Affairs, and Ministry of Law and Justice. The posts include Assistant Soil Chemist, Prosecutor (SFIO), six specialities of Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor, Deputy Assistant Director (Entomology), Entomologist (CCCO), Junior Scientific Officer (Toxicology) and Superintendent (Legal). Onli

important_dates.date for determining eligibility: Closing date of the online application (31 July 2026)
important_dates.recruitment test date (where applicable): Not specified in the notification - to be intimated separately
important_dates.interview_date: Not specified in the notification - will be intimated separately to shortlisted candidates

application_fee.details: Fee can be paid online using Visa/Master/RuPay Credit/Debit Card, UPI, or internet banking of any bank, up to the closing date and time. Fee once paid is not refunded under any circumstance and cannot be adjusted against any other examination or selection. Applications submitted without the prescribed fee (where applicable) are summarily rejected.

age_limit.details: For every post, the age is further relaxable by up to 10 years for PwBD candidates, subject to the post being identified as suitable for the relevant category of disability, and subject to the maximum age of the applicant on the closing date not exceeding 56 years. Additional relaxations apply for SC/ST candidates (up to 5 years), OBC candidates (up to 3 years), Ex-Servicemen and Commissioned Officers (5 years, subject to conditions), and Central/UT Government servants (up to 5 years), as detailed in the "Concessions & Relaxations" section of the notification. The date for determining age and all other eligibility is the closing date for submission of the online application, i.e., 31 July 2026.

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: Master's Degree in Agriculture or Agricultural Chemistry with specialisation in Soil Chemistry, Analytical Chemistry or Soil Science from a recognised University/Institute
Graduate in any discipline plus LLB from a recognised University/Institute; OR Integrated 5-year Law degree from a recognised University/Institute

post_wise_qualification.post_name: Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor (Clinical Haematology)
post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: Recognised MBBS degree plus PG degree/diploma in Clinical Haematology (DM/DNB Clinical Haematology, or MD Medicine/Paediatrics/Pathology with 2 years' special training in Clinical Haematology, or equivalent DNB combinations)
MBBS plus PG degree/diploma in Endocrinology (DM/DNB Endocrinology, or MD/DNB Medicine or Paediatrics with 2 years' special training in Endocrinology)
MBBS plus PG degree/diploma in Neonatology (DM/DNB Neonatology, or MD/DNB Paediatrics with 2 years' special training in Neonatology)

post_wise_qualification.post_name: Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor (Nuclear Medicine)
post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: MBBS plus PG degree/diploma in Nuclear Medicine (DM/DNB Nuclear Medicine, MD/DNB Radio-Therapy or Radio-Diagnosis with 2 years' experience in Nuclear Medicine, MD/DNB Medicine with Diploma in Radiation/Nuclear Medicine, or MD Bio-Physics with Diploma in Radiation/Nuclear Medicine)

post_wise_qualification.post_name: Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor (Pulmonary Medicine)
post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: MBBS plus PG degree/diploma in Pulmonary Medicine (DM/DNB Pulmonary Medicine, or MD/DNB Respiratory Medicine, General Medicine or Paediatrics with 2 years' special training in Pulmonary Medicine)

post_wise_qualification.post_name: Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor (Paediatric Cardiology)
post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: MBBS plus PG degree/diploma in Paediatric Cardiology or Cardiology (DM/DNB Paediatric Cardiology, DM/DNB Cardiology, or MD/DNB Paediatrics with 2 years' special training in Cardiology)

post_wise_qualification.post_name: Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor (Surgical Gastroenterology)
post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: MBBS plus PG degree/diploma in Surgical Gastroenterology (MCh/DNB Surgical Gastroenterology, or MS/DNB General Surgery with 2 years' special training in Surgical Gastroenterology)
Master's Degree in Zoology with Entomology, or Master's Degree in Public Health Entomology, from a recognised University/Institute
Master's Degree in Zoology with Entomology as one of the papers, or Master's Degree in Public Health Entomology, from a recognised University/Institute

Master's Degree in Chemistry, or Associateship Diploma of the Institution of Chemists (by examination), or Biochemistry/Pharmacology/Pharmacy/Forensic Science with Chemistry as a subject in all three years of B.Sc. level, from a recognised University
Bachelor's Degree in Law (LLB) from a recognised University or an institution approved by the Central Government

qualification.details: A candidate must be either a citizen of India, a subject of Nepal, a subject of Bhutan, a Tibetan refugee who came to India before 1 January 1962 with the intention of permanently settling in India, or a person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka, or specified East African countries with the intention of permanently settling in India. Candidates in the latter categories must hold a certificate of eligibility issued by the Government of India. | For every post, the notification states that qualifications are relaxable at the discretion of UPSC, for reasons recorded in writing, in the case of candidates who are otherwise well qualified. Desirable qualifications, where prescribed, are given separately under each post in the official notification (for example, an additional year of corporate/criminal law experience and an LLM for the Prosecutor post, and forensic laboratory experience for the Junior Scientific Officer post).

salary_details.details: The exact in-hand monthly salary figure for each Level is not mentioned in the notification. The notification specifies only the Pay Level under the 7th CPC Pay Matrix; candidates should refer to the official 7th CPC Pay Matrix table for corresponding starting basic pay.

selection_process: Selection is made by the Union Public Service Commission through Recruitment by Selection
which may involve a Recruitment Test (RT) or Combined Recruitment Test (CRT) followed by an interview

depending on the post - the notification does not specify which mode applies to each individual post beyond noting that a Recruitment Test may form part of the selection process. Where a Recruitment Test forms part of selection
candidates must clear the minimum level of suitability at both the RT stage and the interview stage for their respective category. The minimum level of suitability in interview
irrespective of whether selection is by interview alone or RT plus interview
out of a total of 100 interview marks. Where the number of applications is large
UPSC may apply shortlisting criteria such as desirable qualifications
to restrict the number of candidates called for interview. Recruitment Test / Combined Recruitment Test
and Visakhapatnam. The list of centres is subject to change and will be notified on the Commission's website. Candidature at every stage remains provisional

how_to_apply: Visit the UPSC Online Application Portal at upsconline.nic.in/ora.
Complete Account Creation on the portal, if not already registered.
Complete the Universal Registration module to generate a Universal Registration Number (URN), which is common across all UPSC recruitments and remains constant for the candidate.
Fill the Common Application Form (CAF), including uploading a photograph and capturing a live photograph as instructed on the portal.
Upload signature: sign three times on plain white paper in black ink and upload the image as per the portal's instructions.
Fill the fourth, post/vacancy-specific module for the desired post, available only during the notified application window.
Upload all supporting documents against each claim (date of birth, category, educational qualification, experience, disability, etc.) as separate PDF files.
Pay the prescribed application fee online, if applicable to your category.
Review all entries carefully - applications cannot be withdrawn, and no correction or alteration is permitted after submission.
Submit the application and download/print the finally submitted Online Recruitment Application for your own records.
Candidates are advised to apply well in advance of the closing date rather than waiting until the last day, and to keep their Aadhaar Card ready as the preferred ID document for verification.
important_instructions: All correspondence from UPSC will be made through the registered e-mail ID only, including interview schedules and requirements for submission of certificate copies - candidates must enter a correct, active e-mail address. Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) can apply for any post listed as "suitable" for their category, even if that specific post is not reserved for PwBD; such candidates are considered by the general standard of merit unless the post carries a PwBD reservation. Only persons with 40% or more of the relevant disability are eligible for the benefit of reservation and other relaxations under PwBD provisions. Where sufficient PwBD candidates are not available for posts exclusively reserved and identified suitable for them, the required years of experience (not the nature of experience) may be relaxed by up to 50%. Scribe facility is available to PwBD candidates in the categories of blindness, both-arms-affected locomotor disability, and cerebral palsy on request; for other categories, a certificate of physical limitation to write from a Government Chief Medical Officer/Civil Surgeon/Medical Superintendent is required. Mobile phones, pagers, programmable devices, storage media, cameras, Bluetooth devices, and similar equipment are strictly banned inside the examination/interview hall, even in switched-off mode. Face authentication is mandatory for all candidates at the examination venue/interview hall. Furnishing false particulars, suppressing material information, tampering with documents, or using unfair means can lead to disqualification and debarment from UPSC examinations and Central Government employment. Canvassing in any form will disqualify a candidate. Summoning for interview does not guarantee selection; appointment orders are issued by the respective Ministry after selection.

content_sections.heading: UPSC 08/2026 Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: A candidate must be either a citizen of India, a subject of Nepal, a subject of Bhutan, a Tibetan refugee who came to India before 1 January 1962 with the intention of permanently settling in India, or a person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka, or specified East African countries with the intention of permanently settling in India. Candidates in the latter categories must hold a certificate of eligibility issued by the Government of India. For every post, the notification states that qualifications are relaxable at the discretion of UPSC, for reasons recorded in writing, in the case of candidates who are otherwise well qualified. Desirable qualifications, where prescribed, are given separately under each post in the official notification (for example, an additional year of corporate/criminal law experience and an LLM for the Prosecutor post, and forensic laboratory experience for the Junior Scientific Officer post).

rows.essential_educational_qualification: Master's Degree in Agriculture or Agricultural Chemistry with specialisation in Soil Chemistry, Analytical Chemistry or Soil Science from a recognised University/Institute
rows.essential_experience: 3 years' experience in Soil and Water analysis in a Central/State Government, UT, autonomous/statutory organisation, PSU, University or recognised research institution

rows.essential_educational_qualification: Graduate in any discipline plus LLB from a recognised University/Institute; OR Integrated 5-year Law degree from a recognised University/Institute
rows.essential_experience: 1 year (for regular LLB route) or 2 years (for integrated law degree route) experience handling litigation and court matters / administration of law in a Government organisation

rows.post: Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor (Clinical Haematology)
rows.essential_educational_qualification: Recognised MBBS degree plus PG degree/diploma in Clinical Haematology (DM/DNB Clinical Haematology, or MD Medicine/Paediatrics/Pathology with 2 years' special training in Clinical Haematology, or equivalent DNB combinations)
rows.essential_experience: 3 years' teaching experience as Senior Resident/Tutor/Demonstrator/Registrar/Assistant Professor/Lecturer in the concerned speciality after the first PG degree

rows.essential_educational_qualification: MBBS plus PG degree/diploma in Endocrinology (DM/DNB Endocrinology, or MD/DNB Medicine or Paediatrics with 2 years' special training in Endocrinology)
MBBS plus PG degree/diploma in Neonatology (DM/DNB Neonatology, or MD/DNB Paediatrics with 2 years' special training in Neonatology)

rows.post: Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor (Nuclear Medicine)
rows.essential_educational_qualification: MBBS plus PG degree/diploma in Nuclear Medicine (DM/DNB Nuclear Medicine, MD/DNB Radio-Therapy or Radio-Diagnosis with 2 years' experience in Nuclear Medicine, MD/DNB Medicine with Diploma in Radiation/Nuclear Medicine, or MD Bio-Physics with Diploma in Radiation/Nuclear Medicine)

rows.post: Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor (Pulmonary Medicine)
rows.essential_educational_qualification: MBBS plus PG degree/diploma in Pulmonary Medicine (DM/DNB Pulmonary Medicine, or MD/DNB Respiratory Medicine, General Medicine or Paediatrics with 2 years' special training in Pulmonary Medicine)

rows.post: Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor (Paediatric Cardiology)
rows.essential_educational_qualification: MBBS plus PG degree/diploma in Paediatric Cardiology or Cardiology (DM/DNB Paediatric Cardiology, DM/DNB Cardiology, or MD/DNB Paediatrics with 2 years' special training in Cardiology)

rows.post: Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor (Surgical Gastroenterology)
rows.essential_educational_qualification: MBBS plus PG degree/diploma in Surgical Gastroenterology (MCh/DNB Surgical Gastroenterology, or MS/DNB General Surgery with 2 years' special training in Surgical Gastroenterology)
Master's Degree in Zoology with Entomology, or Master's Degree in Public Health Entomology, from a recognised University/Institute
rows.essential_experience: 3 years' experience in teaching and/or research and/or control in Medical Entomology, Public Health Entomology or Vector Borne Diseases under a Central/State Government body, autonomous/statutory organisation, PSU, University or recognised research institute

rows.essential_educational_qualification: Master's Degree in Zoology with Entomology as one of the papers, or Master's Degree in Public Health Entomology, from a recognised University/Institute
rows.essential_experience: 3 years' experience in teaching and/or research and/or control in Medical Entomology, Public Health Entomology or Vector Borne Diseases

rows.essential_educational_qualification: Master's Degree in Chemistry, or Associateship Diploma of the Institution of Chemists (by examination), or Biochemistry/Pharmacology/Pharmacy/Forensic Science with Chemistry as a subject in all three years of B.Sc. level, from a recognised University
rows.essential_experience: 3 years' experience in research and analytical work in Toxicology in a Central/State organisation, recognised institute, university, or forensic science laboratory

rows.essential_educational_qualification: Bachelor's Degree in Law (LLB) from a recognised University or an institution approved by the Central Government
rows.essential_experience: 2 years' experience as an Officer of the State Judicial Service, OR in the Legal Department of a State, OR as a Central Government servant in legal affairs, OR as an Advocate at the Bar

content_sections.heading: UPSC Advt 08/2026 Age Limit
content_sections.text: For every post, the age is further relaxable by up to 10 years for PwBD candidates, subject to the post being identified as suitable for the relevant category of disability, and subject to the maximum age of the applicant on the closing date not exceeding 56 years. Additional relaxations apply for SC/ST candidates (up to 5 years), OBC candidates (up to 3 years), Ex-Servicemen and Commissioned Officers (5 years, subject to conditions), and Central/UT Government servants (up to 5 years), as detailed in the "Concessions & Relaxations" section of the notification. The date for determining age and all other eligibility is the closing date for submission of the online application, i.e., 31 July 2026.

content_sections.heading: UPSC 08/2026 Salary / Pay Scale
content_sections.text: The exact in-hand monthly salary figure for each Level is not mentioned in the notification. The notification specifies only the Pay Level under the 7th CPC Pay Matrix; candidates should refer to the official 7th CPC Pay Matrix table for corresponding starting basic pay.

content_sections.heading: UPSC Advt 08/2026 Application Fee
content_sections.text: Fee can be paid online using Visa/Master/RuPay Credit/Debit Card, UPI, or internet banking of any bank, up to the closing date and time. Fee once paid is not refunded under any circumstance and cannot be adjusted against any other examination or selection. Applications submitted without the prescribed fee (where applicable) are summarily rejected.

content_sections.heading: UPSC 08/2026 Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection is made by the Union Public Service Commission through Recruitment by Selection, which may involve a Recruitment Test (RT) or Combined Recruitment Test (CRT) followed by an interview, or interview only, depending on the post - the notification does not specify which mode applies to each individual post beyond noting that a Recruitment Test may form part of the selection process. Where a Recruitment Test forms part of selection, candidates must clear the minimum level of suitability at both the RT stage and the interview stage for their respective category. The minimum level of suitability in interview, irrespective of whether selection is by interview alone or RT plus interview, is: UR – 50 marks, OBC – 45 marks, SC/ST/PH – 40 marks, out of a total of 100 interview marks. Where the number of applications is large, UPSC may apply shortlisting criteria such as desirable qualifications, higher educational qualifications, higher relevant experience, or a Recruitment Test, to restrict the number of candidates called for interview. Recruitment Test / Combined Recruitment Test, where held, is ordinarily conducted at: Ahmedabad, Bhopal, Chennai, Delhi, Dispur (Guwahati), Jaipur, Jammu, Kochi, Kolkata, Lucknow, Mumbai, Nagpur, Port Blair, Ranchi, and Visakhapatnam. The list of centres is subject to change and will be notified on the Commission's website. Candidature at every stage remains provisional, subject to the candidate satisfying all prescribed eligibility conditions.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for UPSC Advt 08/2026
content_sections.text: Matriculation/10th Standard certificate or equivalent mark sheet indicating date of birth Degree/Diploma certificate (or provisional certificate with all mark sheets) as proof of educational qualification claimed Order/letter for any equivalent educational qualification claimed, naming the authority and its number/date Experience certificate(s) in the prescribed proforma from the Head of the concerned Organisation/Department, mentioning duration, basic pay/consolidated pay and nature of duties Caste certificate in the prescribed proforma for SC/ST/OBC candidates seeking reservation Non-creamy-layer declaration for OBC candidates, in addition to the OBC certificate Certificate of Disability in the prescribed proforma, issued by a Medical Board constituted by the Central or State Government, for PwBD candidates Documents relating to change of name, if applicable (marriage/divorce/other, as specified in the notification) Age relaxation certificates for Ex-Servicemen, Commissioned Officers, Central/UT Government employees, or other special provisions, as applicable Certificates for any claim of Professional Registration, Language, Publications, NET, GATE, Conference or Internship Declaration by candidates already in regular Government service that they have informed their Head of Office/Department of having applied Documents such as pay slips, resumes, appointment letters, relieving letters, or unsigned experience certificates must not be uploaded in the Document Upload Module. If any document is in a language other than Hindi or English, an attested/notarised transcript must be submitted.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for UPSC Advertisement No. 08/2026
content_sections.text: Visit the UPSC Online Application Portal at upsconline.nic.in/ora . Complete Account Creation on the portal, if not already registered. Complete the Universal Registration module to generate a Universal Registration Number (URN), which is common across all UPSC recruitments and remains constant for the candidate. Fill the Common Application Form (CAF), including uploading a photograph and capturing a live photograph as instructed on the portal. Upload signature: sign three times on plain white paper in black ink and upload the image as per the portal's instructions. Fill the fourth, post/vacancy-specific module for the desired post, available only during the notified application window. Upload all supporting documents against each claim (date of birth, category, educational qualification, experience, disability, etc.) as separate PDF files. Pay the prescribed application fee online, if applicable to your category. Review all entries carefully - applications cannot be withdrawn, and no correction or alteration is permitted after submission. Submit the application and download/print the finally submitted Online Recruitment Application for your own records. Candidates are advised to apply well in advance of the closing date rather than waiting until the last day, and to keep their Aadhaar Card ready as the preferred ID document for verification.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: All correspondence from UPSC will be made through the registered e-mail ID only, including interview schedules and requirements for submission of certificate copies - candidates must enter a correct, active e-mail address. Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) can apply for any post listed as "suitable" for their category, even if that specific post is not reserved for PwBD; such candidates are considered by the general standard of merit unless the post carries a PwBD reservation. Only persons with 40% or more of the relevant disability are eligible for the benefit of reservation and other relaxations under PwBD provisions. Where sufficient PwBD candidates are not available for posts exclusively reserved and identified suitable for them, the required years of experience (not the nature of experience) may be relaxed by up to 50%. Scribe facility is available to PwBD candidates in the categories of blindness, both-arms-affected locomotor disability, and cerebral palsy on request; for other categories, a certificate of physical limitation to write from a Government Chief Medical Officer/Civil Surgeon/Medical Superintendent is required. Mobile phones, pagers, programmable devices, storage media, cameras, Bluetooth devices, and similar equipment are strictly banned inside the examination/interview hall, even in switched-off mode. Face authentication is mandatory for all candidates at the examination venue/interview hall. Furnishing false particulars, suppressing material information, tampering with documents, or using unfair means can lead to disqualification and debarment from UPSC examinations and Central Government employment. Canvassing in any form will disqualify a candidate. Summoning for interview does not guarantee selection; appointment orders are issued by the respective Ministry after selection.

content_sections.heading: UPSC Advertisement 08/2026 Important Links
content_sections.text: Apply Online: Click here Official Notification PDF : Click here Official Website: Click here Join Arattai Channel: Click Here Join Telegram Channel: Click Here Join WhatsApp Channel : Click Here Sarkari Result : Click Here Download Mobile App: Click Here

faq.question: Q1. What is UPSC Advertisement No. 08/2026?
faq.answer: It is an online recruitment notification issued by the Union Public Service Commission for filling 13 different posts by selection, spanning ministries such as Agriculture, Corporate Affairs, Health and Family Welfare, Home Affairs, and Law and Justice.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for UPSC Advt 08/2026?
faq.answer: The online application closes at 1800 hrs on 31 July 2026. Applications open on 11 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are there in UPSC Advertisement 08/2026?
faq.answer: There are 13 posts with vacancy counts ranging from 1 to 13 per post, as listed in the Vacancy Details table above.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for UPSC Advt 08/2026?
faq.answer: The fee is Rs. 25/- for UR/EWS/OBC male candidates. Female candidates of any community, and SC/ST/PwBD candidates, are exempted from the fee.

faq.question: Q5. Is there age relaxation for PwBD candidates in this recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes. PwBD candidates get an age relaxation of up to 10 years for posts identified as suitable for their disability category, whether or not the post is formally reserved for PwBD, subject to a maximum age of 56 years on the closing date.

faq.question: Q6. Can a PwBD candidate apply for a post that is not reserved for PwBD?
faq.answer: Yes, if the post has been identified as "suitable" for the relevant disability category in the notification. Such candidates are considered for selection by the general standard of merit rather than under reservation, unless the specific post also carries a PwBD reservation.

faq.question: Q7. Is there a Recruitment Test for these posts?
faq.answer: The notification indicates that a Recruitment Test (RT) or Combined Recruitment Test (CRT) may form part of the selection process for some posts, followed by an interview. The notification does not specify, post by post, which posts will have an RT; this detail is not mentioned in the source document and should be confirmed from UPSC's post-specific communication.

faq.question: Q8. Can I edit or withdraw my application after submission?
faq.answer: No. The notification clearly states that candidates cannot withdraw their application after submission, and no correction, alteration or modification of any field is allowed once the form is submitted.

faq.question: Q9. Which document should be used for ID verification?
faq.answer: Candidates are strongly advised to use their Aadhaar Card, though a Voter Card (EPIC), PAN Card, Passport, Driving License or any other Government-issued Photo ID Card is also accepted.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I get help if I face issues while applying?
faq.answer: Candidates can contact the UPSC helpline at 011-24041001 or 011-40303444, or e-mail [email protected], between 10:00 AM and 5:30 PM on working days during the application window.

title: UPSC Recruitment for Assistant Soil Chemist, Prosecutor, Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor & 10 Other Posts – Apply Online
